comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the Ichinichi app, with users sharing their thoughts on its features, suggesting improvements, and discussing related tools. Many comments express enthusiasm for the app's design and concept, while others inquire about specific functionalities and share personal experiences with similar applications.

comments.author: redgridtactical
comments.text: The read-only past is a really smart design choice. I build local-first apps and it's always tempting to add edit-everything...
comments.analysis: This comment provides a thoughtful insight into the design choices of the app and reflects the author's experience in building similar applications.

comments.author: thatgurjot
comments.text: Love it! The name, the design, the concept, the open source codebase, everything! It’s less like a note taking app and more like a diary...
comments.analysis: The comment expresses strong positive emotions and provides constructive feedback, highlighting the app's unique qualities.

comments.author: kaz-inc
comments.text: I really like the idea, and I've actually built something similar. Please format the writing in the post sound less gpt-esque; I believe...
comments.analysis: This comment is constructive, offering a suggestion for improvement based on the author's own experience, though it could be perceived as slightly critical.

comments.author: lxgr
comments.text: A website where "sign in" is featured more prominently than "sign up"? You have my attention!
comments.analysis: The comment is clever and highlights an interesting design choice, but lacks depth in terms of constructive feedback.

comments.author: jcynix
comments.text: Nice, and I like the idea that the past is fixed, but ... is there a way to define the point of rollover to the next day? My "days"...
comments.analysis: This comment raises a valid question about functionality, but it does not provide much insight or feedback on the app itself.

comments.author: thomasfrank09
comments.text: Very cool! I'm curious as to why you removed ProseMirror after trying it out. I've been building my own writing app for a different...
comments.analysis: While the comment shows curiosity, it focuses more on the author's own experience rather than providing feedback on the app.

comments.author: lukasb
comments.text: Very cool! Also have a daily journaling app, hoping the space grows. I've gotten far more value out of journaling than I have out of note-taking.
comments.analysis: This comment expresses enthusiasm but lacks specific feedback or suggestions related to the Ichinichi app.

comments.author: elxr
comments.text:
How does the E2E work in terms of user flow? I assume a you need a password?

Do you need to enter the password every time you open this?
comments.analysis: The comment poses questions about the app's functionality but does not contribute to the overall discussion meaningfully.

comments.author: tomekw
comments.text: For this purpose I wrote an app called Five Years Back: I can write one entry daily, but I can see what I wrote on this day for the past 5 years...
comments.analysis: While sharing personal experience is valuable, the comment does not directly engage with the Ichinichi app's features.

comments.author: dr_kiszonka
comments.text:
That divider with a time stamp on the right is very cute!

I am looking for, in a sense, the opposite of this app. I need an AI-powered IDE-like...
comments.analysis: The comment is somewhat off-topic, focusing on a different need rather than engaging with the app's features or benefits.
